talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Dresden / University of Heidelberg
talk_metadata.talk_title: Multimodal and Agentic AI in Biomarker Discovery
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I wish I were, but I'm not. Jakob could not make it today..."
Speaker said: "AI is no longer the future, it's happening today."
Speaker said: "We cannot just like previously in AI, have one system that produces one output. We have to look at the procedures that the tool calls..."
Speaker said: "Agents are of course susceptible to many pitfalls. There's of course hallucinations, many other potential problems."

key_data_gaps: No prospective validation of Class C biomarkers

crispro_opportunity.description: Class C AI-driven biomarkers lack standardized validation frameworks and prospective clinical utility data, creating an opening for CrisPRO to provide rigorous multi‑cohort validation pipelines.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "What we need is validation in multiple data sets that are fit for purpose... we cannot bypass the levels of evidence that are required."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate and benchmark reproducible multimodal biomarker models across independent datasets, delivering the required evidence of clinical utility.

unresolved_questions: How will Class C biomarkers achieve regulatory acceptance without a gold‑standard reference?
What quantitative thresholds will define clinical utility for AI‑derived outcome predictors?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AI‑Enabled End‑to‑End Drug Discovery at Recursion
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We went from target identification to IND‑enabling studies in just under 18 months."
Speaker said: "We only needed about 200 molecules synthesized for RBM39 and 136 for CDK7, demonstrating speed."
Speaker said: "We are not selecting patients based on CDK7 expression yet, but we are continuously evaluating it."
Speaker said: "We used Tempus data to establish a causal relationship between CDK7 expression and poor prognosis."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential compensatory activity of CDK12/13 not fully explored

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: RBM39 degradation in MSI‑high / DDR‑deficient tumors
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all MSI‑high tumors without stratifying by specific DDR gene alterations
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: DDR mutation status (e.g., BRCA1/2, ATM) as a co‑enrollment biomarker
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small early‑phase cohort may be underpowered to detect differential response

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad advanced solid tumor enrollment with optional ovarian cohort, lacking prospective CDK7‑high selection
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline CDK7 expression for patient selection
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential dilution of signal due to mixed tumor types

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Recursion's proprietary phenomap and AI‑guided design pipelines
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the platform relies on external data partnerships (Tempus, Amsterdam UMC) and does not claim exclusive chemistry, suggesting potential replicability by competitors with similar data access.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad enrollment strategies in early trials may dilute biomarker‑driven signal.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker highlighted rapid AI‑driven molecule optimization (136 compounds in 12 months) but also admitted that patient selection for CDK7 is still not biomarker‑driven, creating a mismatch between claimed precision and current trial design.

key_data_gaps: No randomized control arm for REC‑1245 or REC‑617
Limited long‑term safety and efficacy data

crispro_opportunity.description: REC‑617 lacks a prospective CDK7‑high patient selection biomarker; CrisPRO can develop and validate a multimodal CDK7 expression signature.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We are not selecting patients based on CDK7 expression, but it's something we're continuously evaluating."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate transcriptomic, proteomic, and imaging data to generate a robust CDK7‑high classifier for trial enrichment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Potential compensatory CDK12/13 activity when RBM39 is degraded; CrisPRO can model splice‑factor network rewiring to anticipate resistance.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We cannot share data on compensatory mechanisms right now, but we are always mining for them."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s network‑level resistance modeling can predict emergent splice‑factor dependencies and suggest combination partners.

crispro_opportunity.description: DAHLIA trial could benefit from co‑enrollment of DDR‑mutated biomarkers to sharpen the efficacy signal.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We saw a trend that models with higher genomic instability were more sensitive to REC‑1245."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate enrichment strategies and power calculations for DDR‑based sub‑cohorts.

cited_competitors.name: Tempus
cited_competitors.context: Provided thousands of patient datasets for causal inference in CDK7 analysis

cited_competitors.name: Amsterdam UMC registry
cited_competitors.context: Supplied natural history data for FAP to contextualize REC‑4881 trial

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal DDR biomarker panel to enrich for REC‑1245 response?
Will prospective CDK7 expression selection improve REC‑617 efficacy?

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "AI is no longer the future, it's our reality."
Speaker said: "We moved from handcrafted annotations to self‑supervision on raw multimodal data."
Speaker said: "Regulators now focus on extensive validation rather than mandatory explainability."
Speaker said: "We reduced pathologist annotation time from 40,000 hours to four and a half, even to zero, using foundation models."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential over‑reliance on model predictions without mechanistic explainability may hinder regulatory acceptance
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted that earlier models required 40,000 pathologist hours, implying high resource burden and possible hidden costs.

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker emphasized partnerships (Tempus, Pathos, Model AI) rather than in‑house model ownership, suggesting potential dependence on external platforms.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad patient‑selection models risk diluting signal if not tightly stratified.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker described a highly accurate AI pathology biomarker that received breakthrough device designation, yet simultaneously warned that “black‑box” concerns remain and regulators still demand extensive validation.

key_data_gaps: Limited public data on performance of multimodal models across diverse tumor types
No disclosed prospective clinical trial outcomes linked to the AI biomarkers

crispro_opportunity.description: AstraZeneca’s pathology AI models still require large annotation efforts; CrisPRO can provide zero‑annotation foundation models to further reduce human effort.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We went from 40,000 hours of pathologist annotation to four and a half, even to zero, using foundation models."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s self‑supervised multimodal pipeline can generate high‑fidelity tissue embeddings without any manual labeling, offering a competitive advantage.

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for parsimonious multimodal biomarker sets to identify the largest responsive patient population.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We are developing the smallest set of parameters that would be required and sufficient for us to identify the largest population that would benefit."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can perform feature selection across imaging, omics, and clinical data to define minimal yet predictive biomarker panels.

cited_competitors.name: Tempus
cited_competitors.context: Partner for multimodal oncology data to pre‑train foundation models

unresolved_questions: How will AstraZeneca integrate time‑dimension data into multimodal embeddings at scale?
What validation thresholds will regulators accept for black‑box multimodal models?
